hook: AI gave you a 5-paragraph essay when you needed a one-liner. Again.
caption:
AI gave you a 5-paragraph essay when you needed a one-liner. Again.

Here's what's actually happening:

AI defaults to 'thorough' because it doesn't know if you're a PhD researcher or someone who just needs a quick answer before a meeting starts in 4 minutes.

So it hedges. It over-explains. It gives you the Wikipedia version when you wanted the group chat version.

The fix isn't typing 'be concise' every single time (exhausting). The fix is AI that already knows you scan, not read. That you want the answer first, context only if something's confusing. That 'brief' to you means 3 sentences max, not 3 paragraphs.

One person's 'keep it short' is another person's 400-word essay.

Your bootfile tells AI exactly what short means to *you* — before you even open a new chat.

No more re-training it every session. No more deleting the fluff yourself. Just responses that match how your brain actually wants to receive information.

Link in bio to build yours.
